core_mechanism: strict superadditivity of a Renyi-type (alpha-parametrized) functional under tensor-product/multi-copy composition — the functional evaluated jointly on several copies strictly exceeds the sum of its single-copy values, boosting a large-deviation (error-exponent) rate even while the associated first-order additive quantity stays unchanged, and the boost is realized by a merely classically-correlated (non-entangled) joint input
brief_reason: The abstract proves the Petz-Renyi channel information is strictly superadditive for every alpha in [1/2,1), giving a genuine multi-copy enhancement of the random-coding error exponent (witnessed by a separable, classically correlated two-copy input) even though the first-order capacity remains additive.
